root_text.an3.21:1.2: ekaṁ samayaṁ bhagavā sāvatthiyaṁ viharati jetavane anāthapiṇḍikassa ārāme.
root_text.an3.21:1.3: Atha kho āyasmā ca samiddho āyasmā ca mahākoṭṭhiko yenāyasmā sāriputto tenupasaṅkamiṁsu; upasaṅkamitvā āyasmatā sāriputtena saddhiṁ sammodiṁsu.
root_text.an3.21:1.4: Sammodanīyaṁ kathaṁ sāraṇīyaṁ vītisāretvā ekamantaṁ nisīdiṁsu. Ekamantaṁ nisinnaṁ kho āyasmantaṁ samiddhaṁ āyasmā sāriputto etadavoca:
root_text.an3.21:2.1: “Tayome, āvuso samiddha, puggalā santo saṁvijjamānā lokasmiṁ.
root_text.an3.21:2.4: Ime kho, āvuso, tayo puggalā santo saṁvijjamānā lokasmiṁ.
root_text.an3.21:2.5: Imesaṁ, āvuso, tiṇṇaṁ puggalānaṁ katamo te puggalo khamati abhikkantataro ca paṇītataro cā”ti?
root_text.an3.21:3.1: “Tayome, āvuso sāriputta, puggalā santo saṁvijjamānā lokasmiṁ.
root_text.an3.21:3.4: Ime kho, āvuso, tayo puggalā santo saṁvijjamānā lokasmiṁ.
root_text.an3.21:3.5: Imesaṁ, āvuso, tiṇṇaṁ puggalānaṁ yvāyaṁ puggalo saddhāvimutto, ayaṁ me puggalo khamati imesaṁ tiṇṇaṁ puggalānaṁ abhikkantataro ca paṇītataro ca.
root_text.an3.21:4.1: Atha kho āyasmā sāriputto āyasmantaṁ mahākoṭṭhikaṁ etadavoca:
root_text.an3.21:4.2: “tayome, āvuso koṭṭhika, puggalā santo saṁvijjamānā lokasmiṁ.
root_text.an3.21:4.5: Ime kho, āvuso, tayo puggalā santo saṁvijjamānā lokasmiṁ.
root_text.an3.21:4.6: Imesaṁ, āvuso, tiṇṇaṁ puggalānaṁ katamo te puggalo khamati abhikkantataro ca paṇītataro cā”ti?
root_text.an3.21:5.1: “Tayome, āvuso sāriputta, puggalā santo saṁvijjamānā lokasmiṁ.
root_text.an3.21:5.4: Ime kho, āvuso, tayo puggalā santo saṁvijjamānā lokasmiṁ.
root_text.an3.21:5.5: Imesaṁ, āvuso, tiṇṇaṁ puggalānaṁ yvāyaṁ puggalo kāyasakkhī, ayaṁ me puggalo khamati imesaṁ tiṇṇaṁ puggalānaṁ abhikkantataro ca paṇītataro ca.
root_text.an3.21:6.1: Atha kho āyasmā mahākoṭṭhiko āyasmantaṁ sāriputtaṁ etadavoca:
root_text.an3.21:6.2: “tayome, āvuso sāriputta, puggalā santo saṁvijjamānā lokasmiṁ.
root_text.an3.21:6.5: Ime kho, āvuso, tayo puggalā santo saṁvijjamānā lokasmiṁ.
root_text.an3.21:6.6: Imesaṁ, āvuso, tiṇṇaṁ puggalānaṁ katamo te puggalo khamati abhikkantataro ca paṇītataro cā”ti?
root_text.an3.21:7.1: “Tayome, āvuso koṭṭhika, puggalā santo saṁvijjamānā lokasmiṁ.
root_text.an3.21:7.4: Ime kho, āvuso, tayo puggalā santo saṁvijjamānā lokasmiṁ.
root_text.an3.21:7.5: Imesaṁ, āvuso, tiṇṇaṁ puggalānaṁ yvāyaṁ puggalo diṭṭhippatto, ayaṁ me puggalo khamati imesaṁ tiṇṇaṁ puggalānaṁ abhikkantataro ca paṇītataro ca.
root_text.an3.21:8.1: Atha kho āyasmā sāriputto āyasmantañca samiddhaṁ āyasmantañca mahākoṭṭhikaṁ etadavoca:
root_text.an3.21:8.2: “byākataṁ kho, āvuso, amhehi sabbeheva yathāsakaṁ paṭibhānaṁ.
root_text.an3.21:8.3: Āyāmāvuso, yena bhagavā tenupasaṅkamissāma; upasaṅkamitvā bhagavato etamatthaṁ ārocessāma.
root_text.an3.21:8.4: Yathā no bhagavā byākarissati tathā naṁ dhāressāmā”ti.
root_text.an3.21:8.5: “Evamāvuso”ti kho āyasmā ca samiddho āyasmā ca mahākoṭṭhiko āyasmato sāriputtassa paccassosuṁ.
root_text.an3.21:8.6: Atha kho āyasmā ca sāriputto āyasmā ca samiddho āyasmā ca mahākoṭṭhiko yena bhagavā tenupasaṅkamiṁsu; upasaṅkamitvā bhagavantaṁ abhivādetvā ekamantaṁ nisīdiṁsu.
root_text.an3.21:8.7: Ekamantaṁ nisinno kho āyasmā sāriputto yāvatako ahosi āyasmatā ca samiddhena āyasmatā ca mahākoṭṭhikena saddhiṁ kathāsallāpo taṁ sabbaṁ bhagavato ārocesi.
root_text.an3.21:9.2: ‘ayaṁ imesaṁ tiṇṇaṁ puggalānaṁ abhikkantataro ca paṇītataro cā’ti.
root_text.an3.21:9.3: Ṭhānañhetaṁ, sāriputta, vijjati yvāyaṁ puggalo saddhāvimutto svāssa arahattāya paṭipanno, yvāyaṁ puggalo kāyasakkhī svāssa sakadāgāmī vā anāgāmī vā, yo cāyaṁ puggalo diṭṭhippatto sopassa sakadāgāmī vā anāgāmī vā.
root_text.an3.21:10.1: Na khvettha, sāriputta, sukaraṁ ekaṁsena byākātuṁ:
root_text.an3.21:10.2: ‘ayaṁ imesaṁ tiṇṇaṁ puggalānaṁ abhikkantataro ca paṇītataro cā’ti.
root_text.an3.21:10.3: Ṭhānañhetaṁ, sāriputta, vijjati yvāyaṁ puggalo kāyasakkhī svāssa arahattāya paṭipanno, yvāyaṁ puggalo saddhāvimutto svāssa sakadāgāmī vā anāgāmī vā, yo cāyaṁ puggalo diṭṭhippatto sopassa sakadāgāmī vā anāgāmī vā.
root_text.an3.21:11.1: Na khvettha, sāriputta, sukaraṁ ekaṁsena byākātuṁ:
root_text.an3.21:11.2: ‘ayaṁ imesaṁ tiṇṇaṁ puggalānaṁ abhikkantataro ca paṇītataro cā’ti.
root_text.an3.21:11.3: Ṭhānañhetaṁ, sāriputta, vijjati yvāyaṁ puggalo diṭṭhippatto svāssa arahattāya paṭipanno, yvāyaṁ puggalo saddhāvimutto svāssa sakadāgāmī vā anāgāmī vā, yo cāyaṁ puggalo kāyasakkhī sopassa sakadāgāmī vā anāgāmī vā.
root_text.an3.21:12.1: Na khvettha, sāriputta, sukaraṁ ekaṁsena byākātuṁ:
root_text.an3.21:12.2: ‘ayaṁ imesaṁ tiṇṇaṁ puggalānaṁ abhikkantataro ca paṇītataro cā’”ti.

translation_text.an3.21:1.2: At one time the Buddha was staying near Sāvatthī in Jeta’s Grove, Anāthapiṇḍika’s monastery.
translation_text.an3.21:1.3: Then Venerable Saviṭṭha and Venerable Mahākoṭṭhita went up to Venerable Sāriputta, and exchanged greetings with him.
translation_text.an3.21:1.4: When the greetings and polite conversation were over, they sat down to one side and Venerable Sāriputta said to Venerable Saviṭṭha,
translation_text.an3.21:2.1: “Reverend Saviṭṭha, these three individuals are found in the world.
translation_text.an3.21:2.3: The direct witness, the one attained to view, and the one freed by faith.
translation_text.an3.21:2.4: These are the three individuals found in the world.
translation_text.an3.21:2.5: Of these three individuals, who do you believe to be the finest?”
translation_text.an3.21:3.1: “Reverend Sāriputta, these three individuals are found in the world.
translation_text.an3.21:3.3: The direct witness, the one attained to view, and the one freed by faith.
translation_text.an3.21:3.4: These are the three individuals found in the world.
translation_text.an3.21:3.5: Of these three individuals, I believe the individual freed by faith to be finest.
translation_text.an3.21:3.7: Because this individual’s faculty of faith is extraordinary.”
translation_text.an3.21:4.2: “Reverend Koṭṭhika, these three individuals are found in the world.
translation_text.an3.21:4.4: The direct witness, the one attained to view, and the one freed by faith.
translation_text.an3.21:4.5: These are the three individuals found in the world.
translation_text.an3.21:4.6: Of these three individuals, who do you believe to be the finest?”
translation_text.an3.21:5.1: “Reverend Sāriputta, these three individuals are found in the world.
translation_text.an3.21:5.3: The direct witness, the one attained to view, and the one freed by faith.
translation_text.an3.21:5.4: These are the three individuals found in the world.
translation_text.an3.21:5.5: Of these three individuals, I believe the direct witness to be finest.
translation_text.an3.21:5.7: Because this individuals’s faculty of immersion is extraordinary.”
translation_text.an3.21:6.2: “Reverend Sāriputta, these three individuals are found in the world.
translation_text.an3.21:6.4: The direct witness, the one attained to view, and the one freed by faith.
translation_text.an3.21:6.5: These are the three individuals found in the world.
translation_text.an3.21:6.6: Of these three individuals, who do you believe to be the finest?”
translation_text.an3.21:7.1: “Reverend Koṭṭhika, these three individuals are found in the world.
translation_text.an3.21:7.3: The direct witness, the one attained to view, and the one freed by faith.
translation_text.an3.21:7.4: These are the three individuals found in the world.
translation_text.an3.21:7.5: Of these three individuals, I believe the individual attained to view to be finest.
translation_text.an3.21:7.7: Because this individual’s faculty of wisdom is extraordinary.”
translation_text.an3.21:8.1: Then Sāriputta said to Saviṭṭha and Mahākoṭṭhita,
translation_text.an3.21:8.2: “Each of us has spoken from the heart.
translation_text.an3.21:8.3: Come, reverends, let’s go to the Buddha, and tell him about this.
translation_text.an3.21:8.4: As he answers, so we’ll remember it.”
translation_text.an3.21:8.6: Then Sāriputta, Saviṭṭha, and Mahākoṭṭhita went up to the Buddha, bowed, and sat down to one side.
translation_text.an3.21:8.7: Then Sāriputta told the Buddha of all they had discussed.
translation_text.an3.21:9.1: “In this matter, Sāriputta, it’s not easy to categorically declare that
translation_text.an3.21:9.2: one of these three individuals is finest.
translation_text.an3.21:9.3: In some cases, an individual freed by faith is practicing for perfection, while the direct witness and the one attained to view are once-returners or non-returners.
translation_text.an3.21:10.1: In this matter, it’s not easy to categorically declare that
translation_text.an3.21:10.2: one of these three individuals is finest.
translation_text.an3.21:10.3: In some cases, a direct witness is practicing for perfection, while the one freed by faith and the one attained to view are once-returners or non-returners.
translation_text.an3.21:11.1: In this matter, it’s not easy to categorically declare that
translation_text.an3.21:11.2: one of these three individuals is finest.
translation_text.an3.21:11.3: In some cases, one attained to view is practicing for perfection, while the one freed by faith and the direct witness are once-returners or non-returners.
translation_text.an3.21:12.1: In this matter, it’s not easy to categorically declare that
translation_text.an3.21:12.2: one of these three individuals is finest.”

variant_text.an3.21:1.3: samiddho → saviṭṭho (bj, sya-all, km, pts1ed) | mahākoṭṭhiko → mahākoṭṭhito (bj, sya-all, km, pts1ed)
variant_text.an3.21:9.3: svāssa → svāyaṁ (sya-all, km, pts1ed); soyaṁ (mr) | svāssa → svāyaṁ (sya-all, km, pts1ed); soyaṁ (mr) | sopassa → soyaṁ (mr)
